Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Watson, ESQ., The Law Offices of Mark C. Watson, Telephone (650) 692 - 4001, Appearing for Defendants.
No other appearance

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRENDA MOORE, BERNICE LASSITER, ADAH BUIKE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Response To Plaintiffs First Set Of Document Requests

The notice appears untimely. The notice was served on October 25 electronically, so it needed to be served 16 courts days plus two additional courts days before the hearing for a total of 18 court days before the hearing. (See CCP 1010.6(a)(4)(B). November 12 was a holiday. So the notice was served 16 court days before the hearing, not 18. As such, the notice wasn't timely. Since insufficient notice was provided, the motion is denied without prejudice to its being refiled with proper notice. The court takes no position at this time on how it should rule if the motion is refiled with proper notice.

Defendants counsel is to submit a form of order approved as to form that complies with CRC 3.1312 and submit by email to Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Defendants Megan Rohrer, Ted Kuster, Shelley Carroll, Laurel Rohrer, Amy Zhou, And Grace Evangelical Lutheran Church'S Amended Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher J. Rillo, Esq. of Jenner & Block for Plaintiff.
Mark Watson, Esq. 650-692-4001 for Defendants Megan Rohrer, Ted Kuster, Shelley Carroll, Laurel Rohrer, Amy Zhou, And Grace Evangelical Lutheran Church.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues and reports they need help with settlement. The Court encourages parties to speak with the Court's settlement officer. Plaintiff concedes to the tentative. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Megan Rohrer, Ted Kuster, Shelley Carroll, Laurel Rohrer, Amy Zhou, And Grace Evangelical Lutheran Church's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' demurrer to all six causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action for breach of contract, conversion, injunctive relief, and accounting and overruled as to the fifth and sixth causes of action for violations of the Corporations Code and Church Constitution. Plaintiffs have not adequately alleged standing for their breach of contract and conversion claims and are given leave to allege their capacities that they assert these claims and the facts supporting their theory or theories of standing. Injunctive relief is not a separate claim, but may be sought as a remedy for other claims. Accounting is not available where "the plaintiff alleges the right to recover a sum certain or a sum that can be made certain by calculation . . . the purpose of the accounting is, in part, to discover what, if any, sums are owed to the plaintiff, and an accounting may be used as a discovery device." (Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 179-80). Paragraphs 27, 28, and 46 allege certain sums of money of which defendants allegedly took possession. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege, if they can do so in good faith, that amounts owed to them can be ascertained through a claim for accounting. The fifth and sixth causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Unlike Capolungo v. Bondi (1986) 179 Cal.App.3d 346, plaintiffs need not satisfy pleading requirements applicable to a negligence per se cause of action. The parties' disputes do not involve ecclesiastical matters. By applying neutral principles of law, the court will not impermissibly interfere with religious concerns.

Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
